transcript.text: And I couldn't figure out how to turn the light on, so I'm kind of sitting in the dark.
I think they're supposed to be automatic.
Okay, that's what somebody said, so maybe ours is just burned out.
Yeah, I think it must be.
Yeah.
It looks like you've got some nice fish wallpaper in yours, though.
It's pretty cool, actually, yeah.
You don't have that.
No, sadly, it's just like a glass wall.
Nice.
It's some lovely, lovely wallpaper that we inherited.
It's just the one wall, though, which is nice.
It's kind of an accent.
Yeah.
Think if it was both walls, you'd feel like you were underwater.
Yeah, if the whole office was that way, that would be a little too much.
So I think Ada was in another meeting right before this, so she should be joining any second.
Did you all take off for the long weekend?
On Monday?
I, I, we, we all worked Monday.
Yeah.
My kids had Friday off, so I'm not exactly sure why.
It's so hard to predict with schools.
Yeah, we got it off last year, but my kids did not get it off.
It's like they get every other random holiday off, but not this one, apparently.
Yeah.
How about you?
Didn't do anything for the weekend?
Well, we had planned a camping trip because we thought our kids had school off, then we just skipped school.
Just didn't happen?
Where were you going to go camp?
Yeah.
You know, we, I live in Vermont, and so we often go just across Lake Champlain to the Adirondacks in upstate New York for camping.
So it's very fun.
It's, you know, nice foliage this time of year, so October's a pretty nice time of year up here.
Cool.
Okay.
Hey, Ada.
Hi.
Sorry I'm late.
No worries.
I was saying you're always wall-to-wall meetings, so.
Yeah, it is.
It's not like it was yesterday, but it is pretty intense.
Cool.
All right.
Well, I know Bailey has some content updates for you today.
day, both in terms of assignments, I think, and also the new content, and then we can discuss the blog, Jason.
Great.
Yeah, that sounds good.
So maybe we'll start.
We have some updated images to take a look at, so I'll just share my screen.
Our designer worked to sort of incorporate some more color into kind of existing style, but adding more color.
She thought, I think with the blues, the orange kind of really popped, and so she did some work on it.

So I just wanted to show you some of the examples that she generated just as a test for sort of those first three articles to take a look at kind of the type of images and see if you had any feedback on sort of the use of color, whether you liked the orange, if you're still kind of good with.
And I can share all these in Slack as well if you want to, you know, take a look as well.
Cool.
Yeah, I do like having just a little bit more color in there.
And given, you know, that we have a couple of different blues, I do like that, you know, those are still incorporated in there.
And, know, the color that's added sort of goes with those colors.
think that's great.
And then, yeah, another thing that, that I, you know, maybe you could go back to one of the screens you showed me.
Some of the ones that I'm drawn to are ones that sort of use the whole rectangle as kind of a canvas versus having a lot of like white space around it.
So, you know, some of those like in the middle or to the right, I do kind of prefer some of those versus like the ones on the left that kind of have a lot of white space around them.
And I tend to kind of like that and, you know, a little more like complexity to the design, I think is nice.
Yeah, that totally makes sense.
And I can make a note of that.
And then, and usually with our, even once this is sort of incorporated into the final pipeline, it will still generate some choices for each article.
Oh, okay.
Which is nice.
Great.
Okay.
That's great feedback.
So we can kind of prioritize those designs that use the whole, the whole canvas instead of just part of it.
Yeah.
Okay.
But yeah, I like the orange too.
I think it looks, it's, it's nice with, like really pops with your, your blues.
It's kind of like, I don't know, maybe it's my screen.
It almost looks like kind of like a gold, right?
I know gold is hard to do on a screen, but it's kind of a nice color.
Yeah, I agree.
Okay.
So that's great.
So I will pass along that feedback about prioritizing designs that use sort of the full space.
And then we'll start adding this to the regular pipeline so we can send updated images for the posts that are already ready to go live.
And then from now on, we'll, we'll use these.
Gotcha.
And then, you know, one of the questions I had about images, just as I was working through mine, the last.
Last few days, is there a particular size, like in dimensions and pixels that you guys are going to kind of, you know, use consistently?
And is that the only size image?
I've been just using one image for everything, you know, whether it's on the homepage or the hero image.
Is that kind of the plan to just have one image?
I believe so, Aida.
Is there any other?
Yeah, I think the header image, right?
Yeah, so right now we are able to provide header images, but we are working on expanding that to, like, include other, like, infographic type images as well.
Okay.
The way it's set up right now, it can all run off just one size, which is, you know, I guess gives us the greatest flexibility.
One thing I noticed, too, I think the images need to be optimized.
And, you know, I don't know if that's something that you guys will do upstream, ideally.
we go.
maybe...
Um...
Uh...
Um,
I think some of the ones that I saved out of the documents were like three megabytes, and obviously that's kind of huge.
So if we could get those just optimized to a smaller file size.
Yeah, and we can do that.
And I think it also depends, you know, often the ones we drop in the images are just so you can review them.
So, you know, when we deliver, you know, especially if we're kind of staging them for publishing, then we can add them in whatever is the right file size.
Perfect.
Okay.
Okay, awesome.
And then in terms of content updates, we've also, I think you have two, you should have gotten emails about two new pieces of content to review, and the next three are on the way.
And we also wanted to take a look at some, we've added a bunch of new blog posts choices into the content OS here.
easy poet

So we did some additional keyword research based on our conversation last week to sort of highlight, look for keywords that highlight Otto's unique capabilities, things like booking flights and hotels simultaneously, like queries around how to book multi-city flights and hotels, topics like, you know, is it cheaper to book these things together, places where we can answer the question and then kind of highlight Otto's unique capability.
So in our backlog, have about 25 new assignments here that are built around those questions and the results of that research.
So I think you'll see like a mix of things here, things about, and there's, and I also want to highlight there's a mix of sort of volumes here.
We have a bunch of keywords that are really low volume, and I think especially around places where people just aren't even aware that the capability exists.
Yeah, right, like things like automating, rebooking.
Overflight or those sort of things, which I think even though they're super low volume, it's, I think, really useful to sort of like build educational content around that.
So I think you'll see some of these are lower volume than the other ones we've worked on.
And that's kind of the reason why.
So, yeah, I think there's about 25 here.
I think, I don't know the best way if you want to review them on the call together, if you'd rather take a look over Slack.
And just, you can comment and approve them there.

But, yeah, it's mostly around like things like remembering loyalty programs, you know, booking hotels and flights together, booking multi-city travel, kind of all those capabilities that we highlighted last week on the call.
Gotcha.
And is there a number that you're targeting to kind of have us prioritize or flag?
Yeah, I mean, I think we have, there's a lot of just articles total in the content OS right now.
And think, you know, the.
The we have approved to start, I think the more we're kind of just building out our runway.

So I don't think there's like a specific number so much as if you go through and highlight the ones that are relevant, then we can kind of, and then understand we can, you know, prioritize upcoming content accordingly.
I felt like these ones felt higher priority because they kind of speak more specifically to autos, you know, more unique features.
And I think also getting comments on the ones that don't feel relevant to you, we can kind of deprioritize that and, you know, learn why.
And that kind of improves our research going forward too.
Got it.
Okay.
I will go through them and try to get a couple dozen or so flagged.
What's the best way, like, I'm still kind of finding myself like clicking in and making comments.
Like, do you have a preferred method?
So I think it's up to you.
Like, you can just leave a comment and say it's approved.
can, you know, if it's people.
Like clients, if they have a specific angle they think would be better, like we can update based on your feedback.
Or if you want to, you can just change the status to approve to start.
There's nothing more you want to say about that.
Okay, great.
Yeah, go ahead, Aida.
Yeah, one thing, Jason and Michael, they have commenting access, so they can just leave comments.
But what I can do that's going to be a lot easier is I'm going to add a column that says, that's just like a checkbox.
And I'm just going to call it approved.
So if you refresh your Airtable, Bailey, you should show up.
Can you just, yeah, go to, if you go to Client Views, like on the left, yeah, and you go to Considering.
Yeah, that's the approved column.
And I think you should be able to.
Just, like, mark.
Got it.
Easy.
That should be.
That's great.
And you're saying we do not have commenting access?
You do have commenting access, yes.
But you need editing access for some annoying reason to change the status of a specific record.
Got it.
If you have comments, you know, we'll take a look at those.
But yeah, that's obviously much easier if you just want to check a bunch going through.
Got it.
And so, yeah, I think that is what we've got for content updates this week.
Great.
Cool.
I know you had a question about images, Jason.
We do want to keep them, like, we don't have a specific size that's recommended, but we do want to keep the file size, like, under, I think it's probably, like, 200 kilobytes.
Yeah.
That sounds right.
Yeah, yeah, because anything larger than that is going to take a long time to load, and it's going to impact.
PageSpeed.
I think if they're larger than that, we can just flag to Katya Bailey that we should just compress them before downloading.
Yes, perfect.
I can do that.
Yeah, that should work fine.
And then I think the last thing on the agenda was publishing, right, Aida?
Yeah, yeah.
So how we, I mean, so the way we do this is we, I mean, we already have access to your web flow.

So what we usually do is we will go in and stage the content, which means like we will put the draft in there, we will add the image, and then you can go in and review and make sure it looks good, and then you can hit publish.
And typically like our goal is like in one or two weeks that you feel comfortable that we can just do that ourselves, you don't have to publish for us.
Okay.
And would you typically publish after each post?
Or would you, do you have a rhythm of like, oh, we always publish on Tuesday?
We'll Thank
I think it always ends up being, a lot of the content always ends up being published towards the end of the week.
So Thursdays or Fridays, if you want, we can schedule, I'm not sure if Webflow, I'm sure Webflow has that feature, so you can schedule posts.
So one is published every day, I think that's fine, but we can also publish content in batches, it's not an issue.
It only becomes an issue when you're publishing over 100 pages per day.
Got it.
Yeah, I'm not too concerned about this, I'm kind of flexible on it either way, and you know, I'm often just rolling out little changes.
You know, Webflow is so easy to publish, it's like, literally just click a button, so I'm fine pushing things out whenever.
Okay, okay, cool, yeah, I think, I think the CMS, like the blog, is it in Webflow right now, the one that
we have access to?
Yeah.
Okay, great.
Okay, so I think, should we plan for staging tomorrow?
Sure, that's fine.
Yeah, okay.
And hopefully we can send Suleman a ticket to just stage everything.
Yeah, so that should be good.
And I think we can always, like, go back in and just, like, add internal links once the blogs are live.
Just to make sure that, like, they're discoverable in that way.
But once they're live, we will also, we also check, like, we give Google a couple of days to see if it's going to index blogs on its own.
And usually it does.
But if that doesn't happen within a couple of days, because your website is quite new, we will go in your Google Search Console and just submit it for manual indexing.
And then the more content we publish, it's going to be indexed faster.
It's just a matter It's just a
It of like getting the ball rolling and getting those URLs on your site.
And Jason, we've already published the first four, right?
Yeah, I just pushed those out yesterday.
Oh, okay.
Okay, perfect.
Okay, so then we can add those to your Looker dashboard, and then we can start tracking performance from next week, hopefully.
Okay.

Yeah, the only thing I would call out, too, about just the way I've set up the CMS, I also, we already had an existing category kind of portion of the CMS, and I added our topic clusters to categories, and so we're tagging them in there.
So not all the topic clusters have been added in there.
That'll just be something that we need to do, but I just wanted to call that out.
Everything else is pretty straightforward, I think.
Okay, okay, cool.
Yeah, I think that works.
I think some of these clusters, they're quite long.
I think we can come up with...
Names that are a little bit shorter, so it just looks, it's easier to navigate, but it's not, not anything major.
Yeah, and some of, we can clean up some of what's in there as well.
I think there were a bunch added in there that I don't know that we're getting any value out of them, so if we need to clean up, that's fine.
Mm-hmm.
Okay, great.
All right, so yeah, we'll move to just update your Looker dashboard ASAP.
And yeah, I'm excited to start tracking your performance.
It's always great to start working with someone who doesn't have any content on their website.
It's honestly one of my favorite setups, because you're really starting from scratch, and it's so easy to track the impact of your effort, because you know it's all content most of the time.
Yeah.
So, yeah, it's good.
All right, cool.
Is there anything else you wanted to discuss today?
I don't think so.
Yeah, just reach out if you have any questions about the...
Nothing from what said.
Okay, great.
Well, thank you so much.
We'll see you next week.
Okay, thanks, all.
Thank you.
Bye-bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync between Otto and Growth X to discuss content updates, image designs, and publishing strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=116.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [New image designs with more color (orange/gold accents) were well-received; full-canvas designs preferred](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=131.0)
  - [25 new blog post topics added to content backlog, focusing on Otto's unique capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=408.0)
  - [First 4 blog posts published; Looker dashboard to be updated for performance tracking](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=904.0)
  - [Staging of new content to begin tomorrow, with a flexible publishing schedule](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=736.0)

## Topics

### Image Design Updates

  - [Designer incorporated more color into existing style, adding orange/gold accents to complement blues](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=132.0)
  - [Jason prefers designs using the full canvas vs. those with white space](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=205.0)
  - Images will be optimized to \<200KB for better page load times
  - [One header image size to be used consistently across homepage and articles](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=295.0)

### Content Updates and Backlog

  - [Two new pieces of content sent for review; three more on the way](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=395.0)
  - [25 new blog post topics added to content OS, focusing on Otto's unique capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=408.0)
  - [Topics include booking flights and hotels simultaneously, multi-city travel, loyalty programs](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)
  - [Mix of high and low volume keywords, including educational content for lesser-known features](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=460.0)
  - [New "Approved" checkbox added in Airtable for easier content selection](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=615.0)

### Publishing Process and Strategy

  - [Growth X to stage content in Webflow CMS](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=736.0)
  - [Otto team to review and publish initially, with potential for Growth X to take over in 1-2 weeks](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=736.0)
  - [Flexible publishing schedule, likely towards end of week (Thursdays/Fridays)](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=773.0)
  - [Google indexing to be monitored; manual submission if needed after a few days](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=873.0)

### Blog Structure and Categories

  - [Existing CMS categories expanded to include topic clusters](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=923.0)
  - [Some cluster names may need shortening for easier navigation](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=924.0)
  - [Potential cleanup of unnecessary categories](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=969.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Jason to review and approve new blog post topics in Airtable](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=559.0)
  - [Growth X to stage new content in Webflow CMS tomorrow](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=736.0)
  - [Update Looker dashboard with newly published blog posts](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=904.0)
  - [Monitor Google indexing of new content](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=873.0)
  - [Consider shortening some topic cluster names for improved navigation](https://fathom.video/share/irqcKHn_ZzhtrPd4W2zayB2gA8f-9QPC?tab=summary&timestamp=923.0)

action_items.description: Share updated image examples in Slack for Jason/Michael
Update image guidance: prioritize full-canvas designs; add to pipeline; send updated images for ready posts
Review 25 new Content OS assignments; approve ~20 via 'Approved' checkbox
Stage next blog posts in Webflow; send Suleman ticket; then Jason publish and GrowthX add internal links
Add first 4 published blog posts to Looker dashboard; start tracking next week
